mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate cuts below 1.5% signal aggressive monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This mechanism operates continuously whenever the policy rate is in the lower tail of its historical distribution, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.97% on 2008-10-01 indicates emergency easing
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return when FEDFUNDS < 1.5% is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate increases exceeding 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month window signal deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger equity risk-off behavior, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice recession probability.
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
fact:UNRATE at 6.5% on 2008-10-01 reflects ongoing labor market deterioration
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following unemployment spikes > 0.5pp are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline combined with housing starts (HOUST) decline below 800k units signals collapsing consumer demand and housing investment. This joint deterioration in demand indicators produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 800k units AND RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
series:HOUST
fact:HOUST at 817k on 2008-09-01 near critical threshold
fact:RSAFS at 363.7B on 2008-10-01 reflects consumer stress
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between joint RSAFS-HOUST deterioration and subsequent equity returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 108 index points signal supply-side contraction and reduced corporate capacity utilization. This mechanism produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as profit margins compress and capital expenditure expectations decline.
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
fact:INDPRO at 107.254 on 2008-09-01 indicates supply contraction
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when INDPRO < 108 are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) declines combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) above 6.0% signal synchronized labor market deterioration. This interaction-driven mechanism produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors recognize sustained earnings headwinds from reduced consumer purchasing power.
evidence_refs: series:PAYEMS
series:UNRATE
fact:PAYEMS at 136.9M on 2008-10-01 reflects job losses
fact:UNRATE at 6.5% on 2008-10-01 confirms labor stress
regime_hint: synchronized labor market contraction during recession
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following joint PAYEMS declines and UNRATE > 6% are positive in > 55% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL below 220 index points combined with FEDFUNDS below 1.5% signals deflationary pressure and monetary accommodation. This interaction produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as real interest rates become deeply negative, supporting equity valuations.
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL
series:FEDFUNDS
fact:CPIAUCSL at 218.813 on 2008-09-01 near deflationary threshold
fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.97% on 2008-10-01 indicates zero-bound policy
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when both CPIAUCSL < 220 and FEDFUNDS < 1.5% are negative in > 50% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth declines below 11,700 billion chained 2017 dollars signals economic contraction. This mechanism produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as recession expectations increase and corporate earnings forecasts are revised downward.
evidence_refs: series:GDPC1
fact:GDPC1 at 11,720B on 2008-07-01 indicates near-recession levels
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when GDPC1 < 11,700B are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) both declining signals synchronized supply and labor contraction. This interaction-driven mechanism produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors recognize immediate earnings pressure from reduced output and workforce.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1% month-over-month AND PAYEMS declines > 100k month-over-month
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
series:PAYEMS
fact:INDPRO at 107.254 on 2008-09-01 shows supply weakness
fact:PAYEMS at 136.9M on 2008-10-01 shows employment losses
regime_hint: synchronized supply-side and labor contraction during crisis
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following joint INDPRO and PAYEMS declines are positive in > 60% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).
